parameters.text:
=Write a professional blog post for CloudElite, a certified Salesforce consulting partner based in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia. The topic is: {{ $json.topic }}
Use this exact frontmatter structure at the top:
---
title: "[title]"
excerpt: "[one sentence summary]"
date: "{{ $json.todayDate }}"
author: "CloudElite Team"
category: "Consulting"
coverImage: "/images/blog/PLACEHOLDER"
coverImageCredit: "PLACEHOLDER"
coverImageCreditUrl: "PLACEHOLDER"
---
Follow this exact content structure:
- Start directly with a 2-3 sentence hook paragraph presenting a relatable business problem. No heading before this paragraph.
- One transition paragraph expanding the problem and introducing the solution framework
- 4-5 numbered main sections using ## 1. [Section Title] format. Each section must contain exactly three ### subsections in this order: ### The Problem, ### Why It Happens, ### What a Consultant Does
- ## Bonus: The Hidden Costs of [relevant phrase] section
- ## The CloudElite Advantage section — position CloudElite as the expert solution. Must mention: 50+ Salesforce certifications, 100+ successful projects, expertise across Sales Cloud, Service Cloud, Marketing Cloud, Experience Cloud. End with [Contact us today](/) as the final CTA link
Tone: authoritative, consultative, enterprise B2B. Target audience: business owners, CTOs, CIOs, sales directors, and operations managers in Saudi Arabia.
Include Saudi Arabia context where relevant — reference Vision 2030 digital transformation, CRM adoption trends in Saudi businesses, and Salesforce ecosystem growth in the region.
Write approximately 1500-2000 words total. For unordered lists use `-` (hyphen), not `*` (asterisk). Output only raw markdown — no code fences, no explanation, nothing before the frontmatter dashes.

=Translate the following blog post to Arabic. Rules:
- Keep the exact same markdown structure, all headings, and all formatting
- Translate frontmatter fields: title, excerpt. Keep date the same. Change author to فريق كلاود إيليت. Change category to استشارات. Keep coverImage, coverImageCredit, coverImageCreditUrl as PLACEHOLDER
- Translate all body content naturally and fluently into Arabic
- Keep all URLs intact — do not translate URLs
- Keep the [Contact us today](/) link but translate the link text to تواصل معنا اليوم
- Do not translate code or technical Salesforce product names (Sales Cloud, Service Cloud, etc.) — keep them in English
- Output only raw markdown — no code fences, no explanation
Here is the blog post to translate:
{{ $json.enContent }}

assignments.name: imageUrl
assignments.value: ={{ $('Process Unsplash & Replace Placeholders').first().json.hasImage ? ('https://github.com/accezcloud-sketch/CloudEliteSite/blob/main/' + $('Process Unsplash & Replace Placeholders').first().json.imageFilePath) : 'skipped (no Unsplash result)' }}

parameters.content:
## 1. Manual Trigger
Click 'Test workflow' to start. Manual only — schedule will be added after testing.

## 2. Read CloudElite Topics Sheet
Reads from sheet ID `1-lSPbE...PV2P7w`, tab **CloudElite Topics**. Code finds first row where col B = `pending`. Stores topic, row number, slug, and today's date for downstream use.

## 3. Generate English Blog
Gemini `gemini-2.5-flash-lite-preview-06-17` produces the EN article with PLACEHOLDER cover fields. Today's date is injected via Code, NOT guessed by the model.

## 4. Generate Arabic Translation
Second Gemini call. Takes the cleaned English content as input, translates body + frontmatter title/excerpt. Keeps PLACEHOLDER cover fields untouched.

## 5. Unsplash + Placeholder Substitution
Search Unsplash → Process Result replaces PLACEHOLDER cover fields in BOTH EN and AR with the same image. If Unsplash fails, keeps fallback `salesforce-default.jpg` and skips the image push.

## 6. Conditional Image Push
If Has Image = TRUE → download binary → push to `public/images/blog/<slug>.jpg`. FALSE → skip directly to article pushes.

## 7. Push Articles (EN + AR)
EN → `content/blog/<slug>.md`
AR → `content/blog/ar/<slug>.md`
Both use stopWorkflow on error — if either fails, the Sheet update never runs.

## 8. Mark Sheet Used + Output
Only reached if both article pushes succeeded. Sets col B = used, col C = today's date. Final node returns status, topic, GitHub URLs.

assignments.name: facebookMessage
assignments.value:
={{ '📝 New Blog Post: ' + $('Clean English Output').first().json.articleTitle + '

' + (($('Process Unsplash & Replace Placeholders').first().json.enContent.match(/^excerpt:\s*"([^"]*)"/m) || [])[1] || '') + '

Read more: https://www.cloudelite.co/en/blog/' + $('Process Unsplash & Replace Placeholders').first().json.slug + '

#CloudElite #Salesforce #CRM #CloudComputing #DigitalTransformation #BusinessIntelligence' }}
